title: My D1 Move in Vlog - Wofford College
body:
Hey bro, I don't know if you'll see this or not but I just wanted to reach out and say a few things.

Dude, you are my favorite tiktoker and my junior year I was saving all of them and sending them to the group chat constantly. You inspired me to pursue college football and seeing how successful you've become, I took it seriously and now as of today have 5 offers. I've also taken inspiration from you and started uploading tiktoks and I'm beginning to work on producing YouTube content.

You're the absolute GOAT bro. I hope you can upload some more stuff very soon.
author: @BigGrapsProd

title: My D1 Move in Vlog - Wofford College
body: To whoever is reading this : May God bless you ! You’re gunna do great in life  ! Put God first and everything will fall into place ! John 3:16 says , “For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life ! Wishing nothing but love , joy , peace, prosperity, faith, hope, etc. Have a blessed day ! God loves you so much 💕
author: @JaniaSanders

title: SC GOP candidates for governor debate in Spartanburg
body: If, God forbid, their was a national / state emergency or covi19 type of situation, who would you want your governor to be?
author: @peoplegetready777

title: Ben Dunlap: The life-long learner
body: Sandor Teszler was my grandfather and I learned a lot more about him after watching this video. Such a humble man
author: @upintheskyx80

title: Ben Dunlap: The life-long learner
body: I was thoroughly blessed to take Bernie Dunlap's Southern Writers course at the University of South Carolina in 1977.  I never knew from one class to the next where his brilliant storytelling and magnificent insights would take me.  After 42 years, I still love him and miss him.
author: @gailzapf9109

title: Ben Dunlap: The life-long learner
body: I am absolutely speechless. Incredible! I love the TED Talks, but this one is by far the best one I have seen!
author: @Rantandreason

title: Ben Dunlap: The life-long learner
body:
I had a similar experience.
Mr.Dunlap's story was moving and inspiring, something I have become familiar with through TED talks.
 I was amoung those that wished roaches would inherit the earth, but knowing such people exist/existed has instilled new faith in me for humanity. 
I'm serious!
author: @InsectRoyalty

title: Ben Dunlap: The life-long learner
body:
God bless you, Mr. Ben Dunlap!
You said, "ez a mi munkánk; és nem is kevés." (József Attila, A Dunánál - részlet).
In English; "this is our job; and not a few. " Verse detail. (At the Danube - poem by Attila József. 1936, Budapest)
author: @peterhodi4955

title: Ben Dunlap: The life-long learner
body: Este vídeo me ha ayudado a motivarme aun más en mi plan de mejora, pero también permite reflexionar a cerca de la educación para la vida. De la importancia de adaptarse a las circunstancias y saber enfrentarse a ellas dándoles soluciones a las necesidades que se presentan en nuestro contexto. Fortalece mi creencia de la responsabilidad que tengo de proporcionarles a mis estudiantes todas las herramientas necesarias para enfrentarse a la vida real y nuevamente reflexionamos acerca de la vital importancia de la CURIOSIDAD en el aprendizaje para toda la vida.
author: @maribelgomez2684

title: Ben Dunlap: The life-long learner
body: When are we ever going to see Dr. Dunlap's Cinematic Eye show again? Somebody upload to Youtube please. Are these forever lost?
author: @OuterGalaxyLounge

title: Ben Dunlap: The life-long learner
body: As a Hungarian I feel really touched and ashamed by this video. I doubt I could ever be such a great Hungarian as the ones he had talked about.
author: @bobebaba3721

title: Ben Dunlap: The life-long learner
body:
My take away from this is that
Make learning process one that even if you're not there your learners can continue to learn and do more for themselves,be able to show themselves to the world.
Human beings are fundamentally good.
No matter what the subject,no matter what the cause even when the keeper of the doomsday clock, he said he is willing to bet even money, that this race won't be around to imagine anything 93 years from now. Therefore;
1) Live each day as if it is your last- Mahatma Ghandi.
2) Learn as if you'll live forever
3) This is our task,we know it will be hard.
author: @abdulrasakkafilat6885

title: Let's Talk: My College Thoughts | Wofford College
body:
I absolutely loved this video! I am also planning on joining Wofford Women of Color and Black Student Alliance.
I also am in a living learning community too 😊
I actually chose Wofford because of the small class sizes and the sense of community. Also I was trying to get out of Florida 😂
author: @solanarostick1470

title: Let's Talk: My College Thoughts | Wofford College
body: Thank you for this! I really wanted to go to Howard to have that culture, but ultimately chose Wofford. Honestly nervous but very excited, especially after talking to poc students and faculty members like Mr. Parks!
author: @nadiaferguson5057

title: Let's Talk: My College Thoughts | Wofford College
body: I love this! I’m going to be a sophomore at Wofford this year and it was most definitely not my first choice of schools either but I’ve ended up actually liking it a lot! Intro to Islam is a phenomenal class btw :)
author: @hannahcambre8878
